title: Reading and Use of English – Part 7
description: You are going to read an article from which six paragraphs have been removed. Choose from paragraphs A–G which fits each gap. There is one extra paragraph you do not need to use.

content.articleTitle: The Enigmatic Life of Agatha Christie
content.text: Agatha Christie is regarded as one of the greatest writers of detective novels. Her works have captivated readers with their intricate plots and memorable characters. [41] Christie’s early life was as fascinating as her books, filled with unexpected twists. || As she entered adulthood, Christie began to focus on her writing career, although her journey was not without challenges. [42] It was during these years that she wrote her first novel, which met with immediate success. || Christie’s fame continued to grow, yet she still faced periods of doubt. [43] This led to her mysterious disappearance in 1926, which remains a topic of speculation. || After her return, Christie wrote some of her most beloved works. [44] Her ability to craft stories that captured the imagination of readers worldwide was unmatched. || Towards the later years of her life, Christie devoted much of her time to archaeology. [45] Her travels allowed her to gather material for her novels, influenced by the exotic locations she visited. || Agatha Christie’s legacy endures, inspiring new generations of readers. [46] Her influence on the genre of detective fiction is immeasurable.

paragraphs.A: In her travels to the Middle East, Christie found new inspiration. The landscapes, people, and cultures she encountered were vividly portrayed in many of her subsequent novels, enriching her already impressive narratives.
paragraphs.B: Despite her initial difficulties in securing a publisher, Christie never gave up. Her perseverance is a testament to the passion she held for storytelling, driving her to create some of the most iconic works in fiction.
paragraphs.C: This discovery took the public by surprise and led to a media frenzy. Many speculated about the reasons behind her vanishing, from personal distress to publicity stunts.
paragraphs.D: Interestingly, she was known to write her novels in a tightly structured format. This meticulous approach enabled her to weave complex stories that kept readers on the edge of their seats.
paragraphs.E: However, it was not merely the plots but also her unforgettable characters like Hercule Poirot and Miss Marple that endeared her to fans. They brought her stories to life with their distinctive personalities and remarkable detective skills.
paragraphs.F: Her debut, 'The Mysterious Affair at Styles', introduced Hercule Poirot, a character who would become legendary through decades of storytelling.
paragraphs.G: The roaring success of her first book is often attributed to her unique prose style and the intriguing nature of her plots. Yet, it was the backdrop of the post-war society that truly resonated with the audience, providing an escape from reality.

questions.explanation: B starts with 'Despite her initial difficulties...' which connects to the challenges mentioned prior to gap 41.
F mentions 'Her debut, 'The Mysterious Affair at Styles'...' which is the specific novel referred to by 'first novel' before gap 42.
C opens with 'This discovery...' which correlates to 'the mysterious disappearance' referenced before gap 43, showing continuation.
E starts with 'However, it was not merely the plots...' which fits after mentioning Christie’s writing ability, adding contrast and detail.
A directly relates to Christie's travels mentioned before gap 45, discussing how they inspired her novels.
D refers to her 'meticulous approach' and 'tightly structured format,' fitting the discussion on her literary legacy.
